title: My CV
text:
Student of BYU-Idaho, Programmer

konakeawekane@gmail.com



I am a student currently enrolled in BYU-Idaho studying computer science. I love cars, RC, sports, and computer programming, however most important to me is the gospel of Jesus Christ and my family. I have come to appreciate the growth and development of computing technology over the years and have grown a personal drive to continue innovating on what can be done with computers. I constantly seek to do things that have never been done before and improve upon the status quo. In several of my own projects I have taken an iterative approach to consistently improve and build better and more capable programs.



Full-Time Missionary

(The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints)

Eagle Scout

(Boy Scouts of America)






Computer Programming

(Javascript, python, rust, HTML, CSS, C#)

3D modeling

(blender)

Game Development

(Unreal Engine 4-5, Unity, Godot, Flax)






Stochastic Viewspace Global Illumination

(Inspired by this paper: https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3451268)
https://konakeawekane.github.io/Stochastic-Cubemap-Global-Illumination

Clay 3D

(A web based 3D game engine with documentation built mostly with Google’s Gemini 3 pro)
https://konakeawekane.github.io/Clay-3D/

Web Audio Spatialization Renderer

(Tool for modeling Acoustic effects of 3D environments on sound files)
https://konakeawekane.github.io/Audio-Spatialization-Renderer/

Custom extensions for the scratch editor

(Integration for compute shaders, Integration for Web Audio API)
https://github.com/konakeawekane/scratch-extentions

Dream Car

(Small web game emphasizing creativity and limitless creation)
https://github.com/konakeawekane/Dream-Car

Rust CPU 3D Graphics

(current educational project)
https://github.com/konakeawekane/my_engine

title: My CV

I have come to appreciate the growth and development of computing technology over the years and have grown a personal drive to continue innovating on what can be done with computers.
